feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Support for multiple user roles in Integration flows sender Adapters (HTTP, SOAP, REST, ODATA etc)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I have a scenario where I have two packages PackageA and PackageB both packages contain Synchronous iflows with HTTP Sender adapter,

PackageA iflows are accessible only via custom role PackageAMessaging.send 

and 

PackageB iflows are accessible only via custom role PackageBMessaging.send 

The reason I have this package based UserRole setup is that these packages are called by individual systems and house all the iflows being used only by one particular sender (so far we didnt have any overlaps between packages)

Now PackageA consumer needs to access another iFlow that exists in PackageB that happens to be an ODATA API exposed via the http sender adapter. Http Sender adapter doesnt have a provision for multiple Userroles and supports only one user role at a time. I want to avoid duplicating this iflow on another http sender endpoint and I want to reuse the existing iflow. How can I do that?

I do not want to share the PackageBMessaging.send credentials with Package A consumer and I also dont want to add PackageBMessaging.send Role in the BTP cockpit instance that I have for PackageA.

entry.title: Cloud Transport Management Service -getting error while importing TR in TMS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When I am trying to import TR Queue from PROD transport node getting below error - 

error during client creation: Forbidden

Also find error from Transport Action Logs-

Exception during start of deployment for deploy type 'SLP_CTS': Error during client creation: Forbidden.

Thanks

entry.title: SAP API Management - Expose Integration Flow Endpoint as API
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am working on exposing an Integration Flow Endpoint as an API using API Management. I've followed several blogs and the tutorial "Expose Integration Flow Endpoint as API." However, I am struggling to get my iFlow endpoint to work with certain policies. Could you please advise if you have a good resource or knowledge base that we can follow step-by-step to achieve this?

Here is what I have done so far:

  1. I added SAP CI as an API Provider using the service instance credentials for the "api" plan. I am using OAuth for authentication. The connection test shows as successful.
  2. My iFlow endpoint is something like https://hostname/http/permit/status. The API should handle one permit at a time and update the status using the PUT method.
  3. When I created the API Proxy, I selected the iFlow using the discover option, and all fields were automatically populated, including the Base Path.

My questions are:

  1. The API Base Path was set to /status, and I added v1 in front of it as a version number. However, I believe this should be a unique path across all APIs to avoid conflicts in the future. Can I change this path to something more meaningful, like permitstatus, considering my iFlow endpoint mentioned above? Would this help avoid conflicts with future APIs?
  2. Do I need to add any resources after this? The tutorial talks about using URL as an API provider but doesn't mention my specific case. If I add a PUT resource, what should the path prefix be? Is it "/status"? I tried using /status, but I get an HTTP 404 error when I try out the API.
  3. Additionally, while adding the proxy, the service instance credentials I used are not the same as the ones I used initially when adding CI as a provider. For the OAuth authentication, I supplied the Process Integration service plan "integration flow." When I test this provider, I get a message that indicates there is an issue. Could this discrepancy in credentials be causing problems with the proxy setup?
    • "System is up and reachable. However, the ping check responded with code : 404; Message : Not Found"
    • However, when I initially added CI as a provider and discovered the flows, the connection test was successful. I used the service plan "api" credentials here.
    • "Connection to the system was successful with response code : 200; Message : OK"
  4. I am also not sure how to check logs or traces to understand why the API test is giving a 404 error. Is there any place where I can monitor what has gone wrong? All I can find are the API analytics dashboards, which show call stats but not the reason for failure or logs. Why is there no monitoring similar to Cloud Integration here?

I feel lost amidst multiple blogs and help links, and I haven't been able to find a single place that clearly explains how to expose this endpoint with basic policies like OAuth, Allowed IPs, Exception Handling etc.

Thank you for your help and guidance.

Screenshots:

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh - Message processing status "Retry" for failed messages
entry.content:
Hello

I have a query on the "Retry" message processing status in Cloud Integration.

When messages polled from Event Mesh using AMQP adapter fails due to some reason during the iflow processing in Cloud Integration, the messages end up in "Retry" status in the "Monitor Message Processing" view. After the message is retried based on the configuration mentioned in Event Mesh Queue/AMQP adapter, the message should apparently go into "Failed" status but it remains in "Retry" status in Cloud Integration.

Is there an adjusment in configuration that can be done either at Event Mesh Queue or AMQP adapter in CI, so that message processing status is displayed as "Failed" instead of "Retry" in Cloud Integration?

Kindly provide your inputs. Thanks in advance.

Regards
Sudarvizhi P

entry.title: SAP Integration - after SOAP request and prepare a retrived PDF the response structure is incorrect
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I apply solution which described @stephen_xue

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/abap-proxy-retrieve-pdf-from-cpi-and-have-it-printed-locally/ba-p/13504044

and generally working (I can download PDF file) but payload's response is not valid with correct structure.

1st payload after request

 

 

?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><tag0:GeneratePrintOutResponse xmlns:tag0="http://tempuri.org/">
  <tag0:GeneratePrintOutResult>
    <__identity xmlns:i="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System" i:nil="true"/>
    <b:_buffer xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System"/>
    <b:_capacity xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">0</b:_capacity>
    <b:_expandable xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">true</b:_expandable>
    <b:_exposable xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">true</b:_exposable>
    <b:_isOpen xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">true</b:_isOpen>
    <b:_length xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">0</b:_length>
    <b:_origin xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">0</b:_origin>
    <b:_position xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">0</b:_position>
    <b:_writable xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">true</b:_writable>
    <a:ErrorList xmlns:a="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/InterfaceSendOrderWCF" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System"/>
    <a:PrintDocument xmlns:a="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/InterfaceSendOrderWCF" xmlns:i="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System" i:type="b:MemoryStream">
      <__identity i:nil="true"/>
      <b:_buffer xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">
        <xop:Include xmlns:xop="http://www.w3.org/2004/08/xop/include" href="cid:http://tempuri.org/1/638520736892059896"/>
      </b:_buffer>
      <b:_capacity xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">96720</b:_capacity>
      <b:_expandable xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">false</b:_expandable>
      <b:_exposable xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">false</b:_exposable>
      <b:_isOpen xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">true</b:_isOpen>
      <b:_length xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">96720</b:_length>
      <b:_origin xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">0</b:_origin>
      <b:_position xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">0</b:_position>
      <b:_writable xmlns:b="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">true</b:_writable>
    </a:PrintDocument>
    <a:PrintFormat xmlns:a="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/InterfaceSendOrderWCF" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">PDF</a:PrintFormat>
  </tag0:GeneratePrintOutResult>
</tag0:GeneratePrintOutResponse>

 

 

2nd payload after prepare PDF as attachment

 

 

<ns0:SalesOrderCreateResponse xmlns:ns0='urn:yourcompany.com:sapecc:lead2order'>
  <Order />
</ns0:SalesOrderCreateResponse>

 

 

3rd payload - in that structure should be a response

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<wsdl:definitions targetNamespace="http://tempuri.org/" xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:wsdl="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/" xmlns:tns="http://tempuri.org/" xmlns:xi0="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2003/10/Serialization/Arrays" xmlns:xi1="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System" xmlns:xi2="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns:xi3="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/InterfaceSendOrderWCF">
 <wsdl:types>
  <xsd:schema targetNamespace="http://tempuri.org/" xmlns="http://tempuri.org/">
   <xsd:import namespace="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/InterfaceSendOrderWCF"/>
   <xsd:element name="GeneratePrintOutResponse" nillable="true">
    <xsd:complexType>
     <xsd:sequence>
      <xsd:element name="GeneratePrintOutResult" minOccurs="0" nillable="true" form="qualified" type="xi3:PrintOutResponse"/>
     </xsd:sequence>
    </xsd:complexType>
   </xsd:element>
  </xsd:schema>
  <xsd:schema targetNamespace="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2003/10/Serialization/Arrays" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2003/10/Serialization/Arrays">
   <xsd:complexType name="ArrayOfstring">
    <xsd:sequence>
     <xsd:element name="string" minOccurs="0" nillable="true" form="qualified" maxOccurs="unbounded" type="xsd:string"/>
    </xsd:sequence>
   </xsd:complexType>
  </xsd:schema>
  <xsd:schema targetNamespace="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System">
   <xsd:complexType name="MarshalByRefObject">
    <xsd:sequence>
     <xsd:element name="__identity" nillable="true" form="qualified" type="xsd:anyType"/>
    </xsd:sequence>
   </xsd:complexType>
  </xsd:schema>
  <xsd:schema targetNamespace="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO">
   <xsd:import namespace="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System"/>
   <xsd:complexType mixed="false" name="MemoryStream">
    <xsd:complexContent>
     <xsd:extension base="Stream">
      <xsd:sequence>
       <xsd:element name="_buffer" nillable="true" form="qualified" type="xsd:base64Binary"/>
       <xsd:element name="_capacity" form="qualified" type="xsd:int"/>
       <xsd:element name="_expandable" form="qualified" type="xsd:boolean"/>
       <xsd:element name="_exposable" form="qualified" type="xsd:boolean"/>
       <xsd:element name="_isOpen" form="qualified" type="xsd:boolean"/>
       <xsd:element name="_length" form="qualified" type="xsd:int"/>
       <xsd:element name="_origin" form="qualified" type="xsd:int"/>
       <xsd:element name="_position" form="qualified" type="xsd:int"/>
       <xsd:element name="_writable" form="qualified" type="xsd:boolean"/>
      </xsd:sequence>
     </xsd:extension>
    </xsd:complexContent>
   </xsd:complexType>
   <xsd:complexType mixed="false" name="Stream">
    <xsd:complexContent>
     <xsd:extension base="xi1:MarshalByRefObject"/>
    </xsd:complexContent>
   </xsd:complexType>
  </xsd:schema>
  <xsd:schema targetNamespace="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/InterfaceSendOrderWCF" xmlns="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/InterfaceSendOrderWCF">
   <xsd:import namespace="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2003/10/Serialization/Arrays"/>
   <xsd:import namespace="http://schemas.datacontract.org/2004/07/System.IO"/>
   <xsd:complexType mixed="false" name="PrintOutResponse">
    <xsd:complexContent>
     <xsd:extension base="xi2:MemoryStream">
      <xsd:sequence>
       <xsd:element name="ErrorList" minOccurs="0" nillable="true" form="qualified" type="xi0:ArrayOfstring"/>
       <xsd:element name="PrintDocument" minOccurs="0" nillable="true" form="qualified" type="xi2:Stream"/>
       <xsd:element name="PrintFormat" minOccurs="0" nillable="true" form="qualified" type="xsd:string"/>
      </xsd:sequence>
     </xsd:extension>
    </xsd:complexContent>
   </xsd:complexType>
  </xsd:schema>
 </wsdl:types>
</wsdl:definitions>

 

 

How can I convert, mapping the response body ?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Zipping Sub File Folders using SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We are trying to create a target Zipped File via CPI Interface. We are able to zip the subfolders which are zipped already. 

However the target expects unzipped file folders to be zipped and sent to them.

Following we are able to achieve 

Target_File.zip >> ABC.zip >> {Multiple Files}

However the target expects the file to be in following folder format.

Target_File.zip >> Target_File (unzipped file folder) >> ABC (unzipped file folder) >> {Multiple Files}

Kindly suggest if such format is possible using SAP CPI Interface. If Yes, Kindly suggest us the ways to implement same. 

Thanks

Tarun T

entry.title: Issue with Updating Records in MS Dynamics CRM via SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
I am currently utilizing the MS Dynamics CRM adapter included with the SAP Integration Suite provided by Rojo Consulting. The scenario involves extracting account credit data from the SAP system and transmitting it to the MS Dynamics CRM di_creditlimit entity. This process should either create new records or update existing records based on primary key fields (di_limitype and di_customernumber).

 

While I have successfully managed to create records in Dynamics, I am encountering an issue with updating records. Specifically, when the primary keys already exist in the system, any changes to other fields are not being updated as expected.

entry.title: Groovy script not able to fetch values from Valu mapping table in CPI !
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have maintained a VM table as below and trying to fetch the values accordingly through the below script. Surprisingly while simulating getting the desired output as SNDPRN (as property) but when triggering from sender then the Groovy is throwing exception as it can't find the value in VM.

Please suggest what could be issue here .

entry.title: Issue on Transportation of roles
entry.content:
In ECC system While transferring roles from Development to Quality and Quality to production system, some roles are missing in production system how to retrieve the roles to production system.

Thanks in advance for your  Answer.

Regards,

Abhiram

entry.title: OpenAPI Validation APIM
entry.content:
Dear all,

i am wondering about a good OpenAPI/Swagger/JML validation. Google APIGEE has implemented already many years ago the OAS Valication Policy. 

There is a Blog that describes a work around but the JavaScript at least throws an error for me that i cannot use it. I tried to debug the problem in the JavaScript but i haven't found why it's not working.

Wondering if there are others also asking that SAP should port the functionality from APIGEE into SAP APIM? I have opened an CEI for it: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#idea/323590 . If you'd are also wondering that this would be helpful for your company, appreciate to vote for it.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Issue Updating Records in MS Dynamics CRM through SAP Integration Suite using ROJO Dynamics adapter.
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

We are currently utilizing the MS Dynamics CRM adapter included with the SAP Integration Suite provided by Rojo Consulting. The scenario involves extracting account credit data from the SAP system and transmitting it to the MS Dynamics CRM creditlimit entity. This process should either create new records or update existing records based on primary key fields (limitype and customernumber).

While we have successfully managed to create records in Dynamics,  we are encountering an issue with updating records. Specifically, when the primary keys already exist in the system, any changes to other fields are not being updated as expected.

Steps Taken:

Developed an iFlow for data transmission.

Configured a message mapping to transform data and handle lookup fields.

Verified that all fields are correctly mapped and identified in the Dynamics system.

Issue:

Records are created successfully when the primary keys do not exist.

Updates are not performed when the primary keys already exist in the system, even if other field values have changed.

Error Message :

A record that has the attribute values Customer Number, Limit Type already exists. The entity key Credit Limit Key requires that this set of attributes contains unique values. Select unique values and try again.

Has anyone experienced similar issues with updates not being performed in MS Dynamics CRM using SAP Integration Suite? What steps can we take to ensure that updates are processed correctly?

entry.title: Balance Carry forward(FAGLGVTR) runs for several years
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am in a project to Upgrade S4 1511 to S4/han 2023. I executed the FAGLGVTR for 2024, when reviewing the ACDOCA table it was executed from 2018 to 2024, placing initial balances in each year in period 0, but the execution What I did was only in 2024.

entry.title: EDIFACT PRIDES message not available in type system
entry.content:
We have requirement to map to Finnish Edifact message PRIDES

UNH+1+PRIDES:D:00A:UN+FI0093'

This is a DESADV extended with segments like MOA, PRI and TAX

This is not available in EDIFACT Type System. Any way to add this in order to use TPM B2B solution in SAP Cloud Integration?

Regards Brian

entry.title: integration suite best practice question for file based integration?
entry.content:
We have an integration scenario where we get a file of 1000+ documents every day and need to perform multiple API calls (Read API to get list of orders, Delete Planned orders and Create APIs). 

Our options are do this via integration suite or set up a backend program or application class. Considering the volume of our input file, does it really make sense to perform this action via Integration suite (potentially taking up to an hour to process a single file). Or is it better practice to send the file to AL11 and then develop the custom object in backend (S4 system). The biggest benefit we see is the clean core with Integration suite, but seems like lot of heavy lifting for integration suite to perform this action?

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite trial: Cloud Integration Capability issue (Error T001)
entry.content:
I'm trying to get familiar with SAP Integration Suite and I can not activate Cloud Integration Capability in my Integration Suite.

The link to my trial Integration Suite https://27b243aetrial.integrationsuite-trial01.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/shell/home

entry.title: Migration tool connection to SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are trying to establish connection from SAP Integration Suite to SAP PO.

SAP PO is reachable from Cloud Connector as well destination connection test is successful.

When I try to add SAP PO by following the blog mentioned, I get the following error.

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/add-sap-process-orchestration-system

 

Regards,

Ramu

entry.title: IBP to Datasphere with mass extraction of key figure data
entry.content:
Hello Folks!

I'm having problems doing mass extractions from IBP to Datasphere. First i've tried the OData APIs, but According to the API documentation: "If you need to extract large amounts of data, we recommend that you do so using SAP Cloud Integration for data services."

 

After digging for a while, however, I've discovered that the service is no longer available on BTP:

What Is the best practice in this case, then? Extract the data using CI-DS? Or should I just go to Integration Suite with Cloud Integration Like in this blog ?

entry.title: how do i implement an odata api for an cds view entity with parameters in integration suite
entry.content:
Hi community

I'm quite new to the SAP Integration Suite and even newer to CDS Views.

What I've done:

I created a CDS View Entity with parameters. That works.

I created an OData-Service with SADL for this CDS View Entity. It works too. So I can call the service via SAP Gateway Client and pass the parameters.

Here the url is like /sap/opu/odata/sap/Ztest/Ztest(p_par='1')/Set

Now, we want to publish an API via SAP Integration Suite that consume the odata service. Of course we have already implement odata services for cds views without parameters and that works fine. But with parameters everything seems to be different. 

If I try to make a request from Postman I tried different URLs. For example:

https://.../gw/odata/SAP/Test;v=1/ZtestType(p_par = '1')/Set           => Requested entity could not be found

https://.../gw/odata/SAP/Test;v=1/ZtestTypeSet(p_par = '1')            => Invalid key predicate: 'p_par='
https://.../gw/odata/SAP/Test;v=1/ZtestTypeSet$filter=p_par EQ '1' => Requested entity could not be found
I'm not sure what I have to in the OData API and how I have to call the service from Posstman.
 
Can someone help me or has someone done something similar?
 
Best Regards
Robin
PS: I  asked google to find something regarding Integration Suite and OData for CDS Views with parameters and found ... nothing 😞

entry.title: "Error Getting Function" during RFC connection in CPI
entry.content:
I've created a Z function and I've tried to call it from CPI but an error always occur:

It is correctly exposed in CC using the RFC name but it would also be available since all Z RFCs are accessible using prefix:

We also have many other Z RFCs being called without any issue. 

I've tried changing the request payload -- even copying from sproxy the sample request -- but nothing changes. I also have found a couple post from the SAP Community but none of them gave me actual solution. Please advise. Thank you in advance

 

Error Details
com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?><rfc:ZRFC_XXXXX.Exception xmlns:rfc="urn:sap-com:document:sap:rfc:functions"><Exceptions> <E1>Error Getting Function</E1> </Exceptions></rfc:ZRFC_XXXXX.Exception>. The MPL ID for the failed message is : AGZaBeDsJhAGLWrtFEOXXXXXX
